rulings.Case Title: JULIE RESLER VS. NADIA FANTA SESAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions; Exclusion Of Testimony At Trial And Motion To Compel Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 5, 2013, Line 1: PLAINTIFF JULIE RESLER'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Exclusion Of Testimony At Trial, And Motion To Compel Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Motion granted-no opposition filed. Sanctions awarded in the amount of $652.50. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH A GAROFOLO et al VS. JON F DOYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 5, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF SYLVIA GAROFOLO'S Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings. Denied without prejudice to being refiled as set forth herein. The motion will be heard by Judge Kahn at 9am, not 9:30am. The only changed circumstances since the stay order is the failure of both plaintiff Joseph Garofolo and defendant Jon Doyle to initiate arbitration proceedings. Based on the positions taken by Mr. Doyle on this motion, Mr. Doyle has waived any defenses he might have to any claims asserted by Mr. Garofolo on the grounds that Mr. Garofolo has not made sufficient efforts to resolve the parties' disputes prior to initiation of arbitration proceedings. If Mr. Garofolo wishes to pursue his claims against Mr. Doyle, Mr. Garofolo should proceed forthwith to initiate arbitration proceedings and in, all events, must do so no later than May 31, 2013 and such proceedings must conclude no later than May 31, 2014. Mr. and Mrs. Doyle must not take any action or omit to take any action that would make it difficult or impossible to meet these deadlines. If Mr. Garofolo does not initiate arbitration proceedings as required or the arbitration proceedings do not conclude as required, Ms. Pastor may refile her motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order with his name and address on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing if the tentative ruling is not contested. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK A. CATO VS. LENAHAN LEE SLATER & PEARSE LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Purported Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 5, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs LENAHAN LEE SLATER & PEARSE LLP, WILLIAM MAJERNIK, JR, CHARLETON PEARSE's Motion To Quash Purported Service Of Summons. Hearing required. The court is inclined to grant the motion to quash service of summons as to defendants Lenahan, Lee, Slater & Pearse, LLP, William R. Majernik, Jr., and Charleton S. Pearce. No opposition filed. The proofs of service of Summons and the Second Amended Complaint on these defendants are defective. First, the process server has not designated a method of service. (Ambrozy Decl., Exhibits A - C.) Second, to the extent that Mr. Cato may be asserting service by mail and acknowledgement of receipt of service, the process server has not attached a completed Notice and Acknowledgement of Receipt for each defendant per CCP § 415.30. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GENE W.Y. YEE VS. EDDIE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Enjoining Deft Fro Holding Himself Out Of Acting As The President Of Yee Shew Yan Benevolent Association, And Directing That Gene W.Y. Yee Shall Serve As The President
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 5, 2013, Line 16, PLAINTIFF GENE YEE's Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Enjoining Defendant From Holding Himself Out Of Acting As The President Of Yee Shew Yan Benevolent Association, And Directing That Gene W.Y. Yee Shall Serve As The President. The motion is denied. Plaintiff fails to maintain his evidentiary burden and does not demonstrate that he is likely to prevail on the merits. Plaintiff fails to show that under the Association's by laws, he automatically ascends to the Presidency. Competent evidence indicates that the clan nominates a member to act as President and an election occurs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order with his name and address n the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
